Decontamination of insulation contaminated with guano (a histoplasmosis source) typically adds $1,500 to $5,000+ depending on attic square footage. Trapping bats is essentially banned in Georgia.

faqs.q: When can bat exclusion be done in East Point?
faqs.a: The legal exclusion calendar in Georgia rules out most of the summer. May through August is the maternity season when non-flying pups are present. The two safe windows are April (before maternity-season activity) and September through mid-October (after pups are flying). Inspections and planning can happen any time of year; only the one-way-valve installation has to be timed.

faqs.q: Is bat guano in my East Point attic dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes. Bat guano supports growth of Histoplasma capsulatum, a fungus that produces histoplasmosis when its spores are inhaled — a real public-health concern when guano is disturbed during DIY attic cleanup. Long-established colonies produce inches of accumulated guano over decades. Professional decontamination uses HEPA equipment and proper PPE; DIY cleanup of established guano deposits is genuinely hazardous.

faqs.q: What do I do if a bat is inside my East Point house tonight?
faqs.a: If a bat is in living space and any person or pet was in the room while it was loose — particularly while sleeping, or with children or unvaccinated pets — the CDC treats this as potential rabies exposure and the bat must be captured and tested rather than released. Confine the bat to one room, do not handle without leather gloves, and call the Fulton County Board of Health or your physician for exposure assessment.

faqs.q: Why can't I do bat removal myself in East Point?
faqs.a: Georgia DNR Wildlife Resources Division regulations restrict bat exclusion during the maternity season — typically May through August — when pups are non-flying and would be trapped inside the structure to die. All bat exclusion in Georgia must use one-way valves, not trapping; trapping bats is essentially banned because the species are protected under both state and federal regulations. Professional East Point contractors hold the required Georgia DNR licensing.

snake-removal.intro: Snake removal calls in East Point run April-October. Typical East Point snake removal runs $150-$500.
snake-removal.extendedBody:
Where Snakes Show Up in East Point

  • Sweetwater Creek corridor — copperheads in adjacent older residential yards.
  • Historic East Point near MARTA — copperheads in dense ground cover.
  • Camp Creek Parkway industrial edges — snakes hunting rodents drawn to commercial dumpster ecology.

Copperhead ID and ER Response

Copperhead: coppery-tan with hourglass crossbands, triangular head, 2-3 feet typical. Photo from 10+ feet for ID. If bitten: ER immediately. Cost: $150-$250 non-venomous, $250-$400 copperhead, $400-$800 multi-snake.

faqs.q: How much does snake removal cost in East Point?
faqs.a: $150-$500. Single non-venomous $150-$250. Copperhead $250-$400. Multi-snake $400-$800.

faqs.q: Are copperheads common in East Point yards?
faqs.a: Yes — documented along Sweetwater Creek corridor and in older Historic East Point yards. Photo from 10+ feet for ID.

faqs.q: I just got bitten by a snake in East Point — what do I do?
faqs.a: Get to an ER immediately. Atlanta-area ERs (Grady, Emory) stock antivenom. Do NOT apply ice, cut, suck venom, tourniquet.

faqs.q: When are snakes most active in East Point?
faqs.a: April-October, with peaks May-June (mating) and August-September (juvenile dispersal).

faqs.q: Do snake repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Mothballs, sulfur, sonic stakes — none reliably deter snakes. Only habitat modification works.

groundhog-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 groundhog removal East Point, GA — under-deck burrows in older Historic East Point. $250-$700.
groundhog-removal.intro: Groundhog removal calls in East Point run April-August. Groundhogs den under decks, sheds, garage slabs in pre-1960 East Point properties. Typical removal runs $250-$700.
groundhog-removal.extendedBody:
Groundhog Burrow ID

  • Burrow entrance 8-12 inches with fan-shaped fresh dirt mound.
  • Common East Point locations: under decks/sheds/garage slabs in pre-1960 properties.
  • Adult groundhog: 16-26 inches, 5-13 lbs.

Pre-1960 Foundation Risk and Cost

East Point pre-1940 brick foundations are especially vulnerable. Female groundhogs whelp April-May; kits dependent until late July/early August. Cost: $250-$400 single, $400-$700 kit-season hand recovery, $700-$1,500 multi-burrow.

faqs.q: How much does groundhog removal cost in East Point?
faqs.a: East Point groundhog jobs run $250-$700. Single burrow $250-$400. Kit-season $400-$700. Multi-burrow $700-$1,500.

faqs.q: Will a groundhog burrow damage my East Point pre-1940 brick foundation?
faqs.a: Yes — pre-1940 brick foundations are especially vulnerable. Repair $500-$5,000+.

faqs.q: When do groundhogs have babies in East Point?
faqs.a: April-May. Litters 2-6 kits; dependent until late July/early August.

faqs.q: How do I keep groundhogs out of my East Point garden?
faqs.a: Heavy-gauge welded-wire fencing extending 12 inches below grade, top loose-bent outward.

faqs.q: Are groundhogs protected by Georgia law?
faqs.a: Not protected as game species. Commercial trapping requires Georgia DNR Trapping License.

faqs.q: Are pigeons protected by federal law?
faqs.a: No. Rock pigeons, starlings, sparrows are non-native and NOT MBTA-protected. Native species ARE protected.

faqs.q: Do you handle pigeon abatement near Hartsfield-Jackson Airport?

faqs.q: How do I get rid of starlings in my East Point dryer vent?

faqs.q: Are bird droppings dangerous?

skunk-removal.metaTitle: Skunk Removal East Point, GA | Spray-Free Trapping | 24/7
skunk-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 skunk removal East Point, GA — under-deck dens in pre-1960 yards. Rabies vector. $300-$700.
skunk-removal.intro: Skunk removal calls in East Point run February-July. Striped skunks den under decks, porches, in older crawlspaces. Skunks are rabies-vector species. Typical East Point skunk removal runs $300-$700.
skunk-removal.extendedBody:
Skunk Den Signs

  • Persistent musky odor under decks/porches/crawlspaces.
  • Burrow entrance 6-8 inches.
  • Cone-shaped lawn digs.
  • Skunk visible at dawn or dusk.

Spray + Cost

Formula: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Cost: $300-$450 single live-trap, $450-$700 multi-skunk family with kit-season hand recovery, $700-$1,500 multi-burrow + exclusion, $200-$800 spray remediation.

faqs.q: How much does skunk removal cost in East Point?
faqs.a: East Point skunk jobs run $300-$700. Single live-trap $300-$450. Multi-skunk family $450-$700. Multi-burrow $700-$1,500. Spray remediation $200-$800 separate.

faqs.q: How do I get skunk smell out of my dog?
faqs.a: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Apply fresh, work 5 min, rinse.

faqs.q: Is the skunk in my East Point yard rabid?
faqs.a: Possibly — Georgia is rabies-endemic. Daytime activity, tame behavior, circling, drooling are warning signs. Do NOT approach.

faqs.q: There are baby skunks under my East Point deck — what do I do?
faqs.a: Baby skunk season April-July. DO NOT seal the den. Den-side hand recovery for kits, then trap mother.

faqs.q: Can I trap a skunk myself?
faqs.a: Strongly not recommended. Spray triggers easily. Bite/scratch risk is real.

$200-$300 single live-trap, $300-$500 mother + joeys, $500-$1,000 multi-opossum + flea/tick remediation, $300-$700 dead opossum recovery.

faqs.q: How much does opossum removal cost in East Point?
faqs.a: East Point opossum jobs run $200-$500. Single live-trap $200-$300. Mother + joeys $300-$500. Multi-opossum $500-$1,000. Dead opossum recovery $300-$700.

faqs.q: Do opossums carry rabies?
faqs.a: Very rarely. Body temperature below rabies-optimal range. CDC treats opossum bites as low-risk.

faqs.q: There's an opossum in my East Point garage — how do I get it out?
faqs.a: Open garage door fully at dusk, turn off lights, leave area, check back in 30-60 minutes. Usually leaves on its own.

faqs.q: How do I keep opossums away?
faqs.a: Eliminate outdoor pet food, secure garbage, close garage doors at night, seal accessible crawlspaces.

faqs.q: Is there a dead opossum smell in my East Point home?
faqs.a: Common East Point call. Recovery $300-$700 depending on access.

mole-removal.metaTitle: Mole Removal East Point, GA | Lawn Tunnel Trapping | 24/7

mole-removal.intro: Mole removal calls in East Point peak in spring and fall. Eastern moles tunnel through East Point lawns. Typical East Point mole removal runs $200-$600.
mole-removal.extendedBody:
Mole vs Vole ID

  • Mole: raised tunnel ridges PLUS volcano-shaped molehills. Doesn't eat plants.
  • Vole: surface runways PLUS chewed shrub bark.

Cost

Sonic stakes, castor oil, mothballs do NOT work. Only trapping in active tunnels works. Cost: $200-$350 single mole, $350-$600 multi-mole, $600-$1,500 multi-acre. Lawn repair $300-$1,500 separate.

faqs.q: How much does mole removal cost in East Point?
faqs.a: East Point mole jobs run $200-$600. Single-mole $200-$350. Multi-mole $350-$600. Multi-acre $600-$1,500.

faqs.q: How do I tell mole from vole damage?
faqs.a: Moles: raised tunnel ridges PLUS cone soil mounds. Voles: flattened surface tracks PLUS chewed shrub bark.

faqs.q: Do mole repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Only trapping works.

faqs.q: How long does mole removal take?
faqs.a: 1-3 visits over 1-2 weeks.

faqs.q: Are moles dangerous?
faqs.a: No. Don't bite, don't carry significant zoonotic disease, don't eat plants.

faqs.q: How much does dead animal removal cost in East Point?

faqs.q: How do I find the dead animal smell?
faqs.a: Walk each room sniffing low at baseboards, ceiling height. Smell strongest at wall closest to carcass. Listen for blowflies.

faqs.q: How long does dead animal smell last?
faqs.a: Severe odor 7-14 days. Decomposition-fluid saturation produces residual odor 1-6 months.

faqs.q: There are flies in my East Point house — is it dead-animal related?
faqs.a: Probably yes if blowflies (large metallic green/blue, 1/4-1/2 inch). Adult emergence 7-10 days after death event.

faqs.q: Can I just leave the dead animal?
faqs.a: Decomposes in 3-6 weeks but waiting costs much more in odor, flies, drywall replacement.

raccoon-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 raccoon removal College Park, GA — Main Street historic district, Princeton Lakes, airport-adjacent. Pre-1960 housing exclusion. $400-$1,500.
raccoon-removal.intro: Raccoon removal calls in College Park concentrate in the city's pre-1960 historic housing — Craftsman bungalows, 1920s-1940s tudor, and mid-century brick homes — with the multi-entry profiles common to Atlanta-pattern older housing. Female raccoons (Procyon lotor) whelp late February through early May, with peak intrusion the first three weeks of March. Heaviest pressure: Main Street Historic District, Princeton Lakes area, Old National Highway corridor. Typical College Park raccoon removal runs $400-$1,500 with same-day humane trapping.
raccoon-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Raccoons in Your College Park Home

Heavy thumping or chittering between dusk and dawn is the clearest sign — raccoons weigh 10-25 lbs. In College Park's Main Street Historic District, chimney scratching is the most common report (pre-1940 masonry chimneys without modern caps). Other signs: damaged soffit corners, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near entry points, urine smell penetrating ceiling drywall. If you hear a baby crying at night between late February and May, that's raccoon kits.

College Park Historic District vs Princeton Lakes

  • Main Street Historic District — pre-1940 housing with multi-entry profiles (4-5+ per property). Atlanta-pattern raccoon work; multi-decade colony establishment.
  • Princeton Lakes area — newer mixed housing with standard south-Fulton entry profiles.
  • Old National Highway corridor — older mid-century housing, multi-entry common.

Cost

$400-$700 single-entry modern, $700-$1,200 multi-entry mid-century or kit season, $1,200-$1,500+ pre-1940 historic with multi-entry + Baylisascaris insulation contamination, $1,500-$3,500 full attic restoration.

faqs.q: How much does raccoon removal cost in College Park, GA?
faqs.a: $400-$1,500. Single-entry modern $400-$700. Multi-entry mid-century or kit-season $700-$1,200. Pre-1940 historic with multi-entry + insulation contamination $1,200-$1,500+. Full attic restoration $1,500-$3,500.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have raccoons in my College Park attic?
faqs.a: Heavy thumping/chittering at dusk and dawn (raccoons weigh 10-25 lbs — "someone walking up there"). Damaged fascia/soffits, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near entry points, urine smell. Entry holes 4-6 inches across.

faqs.q: When can I evict raccoons from my College Park attic?
faqs.a: Female raccoons whelp late February through early May, kits dependent 8-10 weeks. Excluding mother before kits are mobile traps them inside. Right approach: one-way doors after kits are mobile, or den-side hand recovery for very young kits.

faqs.q: Are raccoons common in College Park Historic District?
faqs.a: Yes — pre-1940 housing in the Main Street Historic District has multi-entry profiles (4-5+ per property), masonry chimneys, original wood soffits. Multi-decade colony establishment is documented.

faqs.q: Will the raccoons come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion seals every entry route with welded steel mesh, hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

squirrel-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 squirrel removal College Park, GA — pre-1960 historic housing attic exclusion. Chewed-wire fire risk. $300-$1,200.
squirrel-removal.intro: Squirrel removal calls in College Park run year-round. Eastern gray squirrels (Sciurus carolinensis) dominate. Mature canopy across College Park's older neighborhoods plus pre-1940 housing with vulnerable older wiring runs. Twin breeding peaks (February-March, August-September) drive twin call peaks. Chewed-wire fire risk amplified in pre-1940 Main Street Historic District homes. Typical College Park squirrel removal runs $300-$1,200.
squirrel-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Squirrels in Your College Park Attic

  • Fast scampering during daylight (especially dawn and late afternoon).
  • Chewed entry holes 2-3 inches at soffit corners, gable louvers.
  • Acorn shells on roof or in gutters.
  • Chewed wires — urgent sign in pre-1940 housing.
  • Insulation pulled into nest pile.

Chewed-Wire Fire Risk in College Park Historic Homes

Pre-1940 College Park housing has 60-100+ year-old wiring (knob-and-tube remnants, early Romex) more vulnerable to chew damage than modern wiring. Any squirrel job that exposes chewed Romex requires licensed-electrician follow-up before final exclusion sealing. Cost: $300-$500 single-entry modern, $500-$900 multi-entry mid-century, $900-$1,200 pre-1940 with electrician follow-up.

faqs.q: How much does squirrel removal cost in College Park?
faqs.a: $300-$1,200. Single-entry modern $300-$500. Multi-entry mid-century $500-$900. Pre-1940 historic with chewed-wire electrician follow-up $900-$1,200.

faqs.q: How do I tell squirrels from rats in my College Park attic?
faqs.a: Squirrels diurnal (dawn/late afternoon active), fast scampering. Rats nocturnal, slower scratching/gnawing. Squirrel droppings rice-grain-sized clustered; rat droppings smaller along travel routes.

faqs.q: When can I evict squirrels from my College Park attic?
faqs.a: Two safe windows: May-early June (after first-litter dispersal) and October-November (after second-litter mobile).

faqs.q: Are squirrels really a fire risk in College Park historic homes?
faqs.a: Yes — chewed Romex is a leading cause of attic-origin fires. Pre-1940 College Park housing has older wiring far more vulnerable to chew damage.

faqs.q: Will squirrels come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion uses hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

faqs.q: Do I have Norway rats or roof rats in my College Park home?
faqs.a: Activity in basement/crawlspace/foundations = Norway rats. Activity in attic/ceiling/overhead = roof rats. Pointed half-inch droppings = roof rats; blunt 3/4-inch = Norway rats.

faqs.q: When are rats worst in College Park?
faqs.a: Activity peaks October-December as outdoor food sources disappear. Secondary spike early spring.

faqs.q: Are rats in my College Park home dangerous to my health?
faqs.a: Yes — CDC documents 35+ diseases. Most relevant in College Park: leptospirosis, salmonellosis, hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (DIY droppings cleanup is documented exposure).

faqs.q: Why do rats keep returning?
faqs.a: Almost always because entry points aren't sealed. Durable resolution requires structural exclusion combined with trapping, not trapping alone.

bat-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Bats in Your College Park Home

  • Dusk emergence — bats stream out of chimney top or roofline 20-30 min after sunset.
  • Brown guano staining on siding below entry.
  • Guano piles on porch or attic floor.
  • Single bat in living space.

Maternity Calendar + Cost

May-August maternity season — exclusion traps non-flying pups. Safe windows: April or September through mid-October only. Cost: $1,500-$2,500 modest colony, $2,500-$4,000 multi-decade with HEPA guano remediation, $4,000-$8,000+ historic-home full restoration.

faqs.q: How much does bat removal cost in College Park?
faqs.a: College Park bat jobs run $1,500-$4,000. Modest colony $1,500-$2,500. Multi-decade with full HEPA guano remediation $2,500-$4,000. Historic-home full restoration $4,000-$8,000+. Trapping bats is essentially banned in Georgia.

faqs.q: What do I do if a bat is inside my College Park home tonight?
faqs.a: If bat in living space and any person/pet was in room while loose (especially while sleeping or with children) — CDC treats as potential rabies exposure. Capture (don't release) for testing. Confine to one room, call Fulton County Board of Health or physician within 24 hours.

faqs.q: When can bat exclusion be done in College Park?
faqs.a: April or September through mid-October only. May-August is maternity season — exclusion traps non-flying pups.

faqs.q: Is bat guano in my College Park attic dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes — supports growth of Histoplasma capsulatum (histoplasmosis when inhaled). Long-established Main Street Historic District colonies produce inches of accumulated guano. Professional HEPA-equipped decontamination required.

faqs.q: Why can't I do bat removal myself?
faqs.a: Georgia DNR regulations restrict exclusion during May-August maternity season. All exclusion uses one-way valves, not trapping. Tricolored bat encounters carry federal-status concerns.

  • Princeton Lakes area wooded yards.

Copperhead ID and ER Response

Copperhead: coppery-tan with hourglass crossbands, triangular head, 2-3 feet typical. Photo from 10+ feet for ID. ER immediately if bitten (Atlanta-area ERs stock antivenom). Cost: $150-$250 non-venomous, $250-$400 copperhead, $400-$800 multi-snake.

How to Tell If You Have Raccoons in Your South Fulton Home

Heavy thumping or chittering between dusk and dawn is the clearest sign. In semi-rural South Fulton, multi-structure denning (main house + sheds + outbuildings) is common. Other signs: damaged soffit corners, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near downspouts, urine smell penetrating ceiling drywall. Baby crying sounds late February-May = raccoon kits.

South Fulton Submarkets

  • Cliftondale / Old National corridor — older subdivisions with mid-century entry profiles.
  • Cochran Mill Park area — heaviest source-population pressure from adjacent conservation land.
  • Stonewall Tell Road corridor — semi-rural acreage with multi-structure properties.

faqs.q: Will the raccoons come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion uses welded steel mesh, hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

faqs.a: Heavy-gauge welded-wire fencing extending 12 inches below grade, top loose-bent outward. Soft mesh isn't groundhog-proof.

faqs.q: Are groundhogs protected by Georgia law?
faqs.a: Not protected as game species. Commercial trapping requires Georgia DNR Trapping License.

bird-removal.metaTitle: Bird Removal South Fulton, GA | Pigeon + Starling | 24/7
bird-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 bird removal South Fulton, GA — solar-panel pigeons, starling vent nests. MBTA-compliant. $400-$2,000.
bird-removal.intro: Bird removal in South Fulton focuses on residential solar-panel pigeon roosts, starling/sparrow nests in dryer vents, and barn-property pigeon work on semi-rural acreage. Pigeons, starlings, sparrows are non-native and not MBTA-protected. Typical South Fulton bird removal runs $400-$2,000.
bird-removal.extendedBody:
South Fulton Bird Pest Pattern

  • Residential solar-panel pigeons.
  • Starling/sparrow vent nests in subdivision housing.
  • Barn-property pigeons on semi-rural acreage.
  • MBTA-protected native species require federal permit.

Cost

$400-$600 single residential nest, $500-$900 solar-panel exclusion + remediation, $900-$2,000 multi-vent residential or barn-property pigeon work.

faqs.q: How much does bird removal cost in South Fulton?
faqs.a: $400-$2,000. Single nest $400-$600. Solar-panel exclusion $500-$900. Multi-vent residential or barn-property $900-$2,000.

faqs.q: Are pigeons protected by federal law?
faqs.a: No. Rock pigeons, starlings, sparrows are non-native and NOT MBTA-protected. Native species ARE protected and require federal permits.

faqs.q: Pigeons are nesting under my South Fulton solar panels — what do I do?
faqs.a: HEPA droppings remediation + stainless-steel mesh exclusion around array perimeter. $500-$900 typical.

faqs.q: How do I get rid of starlings in my dryer vent?
faqs.a: Remove nest material, install vent guard with louvered cap, treat droppings. $400-$600. Don't dry clothes with nest in vent — fire risk.

faqs.q: Are bird droppings dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes with significant accumulation. Histoplasmosis exposure during cleanup.

skunk-removal.metaTitle: Skunk Removal South Fulton, GA | Spray-Free Trapping | 24/7
skunk-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 skunk removal South Fulton, GA — under-deck dens in Cliftondale, semi-rural acreage. Rabies vector. $300-$1,000.
skunk-removal.intro: Skunk removal calls in South Fulton run February-July. Striped skunks (Mephitis mephitis) den under decks, sheds, outbuildings on semi-rural properties. Skunks are rabies-vector species. Typical South Fulton skunk removal runs $300-$1,000.
skunk-removal.extendedBody:
Skunk Den Signs and Rabies in South Fulton

  • Persistent musky odor under decks, sheds, outbuildings.
  • Burrow entrance 6-8 inches.
  • Cone-shaped lawn digs from grub-hunting.
  • Skunk visible at dawn or dusk.

Rabies signs: daytime activity, tame behavior, circling, drooling, hindquarter weakness. Bite/scratch/saliva exposure requires physician evaluation within 24 hours.

Cost

Spray formula: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Cost: $300-$450 single live-trap, $450-$700 multi-skunk family with kit-season hand recovery, $700-$1,000 multi-burrow semi-rural property, $200-$800 spray remediation separate.

faqs.q: How much does skunk removal cost in South Fulton?
faqs.a: $300-$1,000. Single live-trap $300-$450. Multi-skunk with kit-season hand recovery $450-$700. Multi-burrow semi-rural $700-$1,000. Spray remediation $200-$800 separate.

faqs.q: How do I get skunk smell out of my dog?
faqs.a: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Apply fresh, work 5 min, rinse.

faqs.q: Is the skunk in my South Fulton yard rabid?
faqs.a: Possibly — Georgia is rabies-endemic. Daytime activity, tame behavior, circling, drooling are warning signs.

faqs.q: There are baby skunks under my South Fulton deck — what do I do?
faqs.a: Baby skunk season April-July. DO NOT seal the den. Den-side hand recovery for kits, then trap mother.

faqs.q: Can I trap a skunk myself?
faqs.a: Strongly not recommended. Spray triggers easily. Bite/scratch risk is real.

opossum-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 opossum removal South Fulton, GA — garage, crawlspace, deck/porch denning. Low rabies. $200-$500.
opossum-removal.intro: Opossum removal calls in South Fulton run year-round. Virginia opossums (Didelphis virginiana) are well-established. Body temperature (94-97°F) is below rabies-optimal range. Typical South Fulton opossum removal runs $200-$500.
opossum-removal.extendedBody:
Opossum ID and Common Sightings

  • Size: 24-40 inches including prehensile tail, 4-14 lbs.
  • Behavior: nocturnal, slow, plays dead.
  • Common locations: under porches/decks, in garages, in crawlspaces, in barn outbuildings.

Cost

$200-$300 single live-trap, $300-$500 mother + joeys (6-13), $500-$1,000 multi-opossum + flea/tick remediation, $300-$700 dead opossum recovery.

faqs.q: How much does opossum removal cost in South Fulton?
faqs.a: $200-$500. Single live-trap $200-$300. Mother + joeys $300-$500. Multi-opossum $500-$1,000. Dead opossum recovery $300-$700.

faqs.q: Do opossums carry rabies?
faqs.a: Very rarely. Body temperature below rabies-optimal range. CDC treats opossum bites as low-risk.

faqs.q: There's an opossum in my South Fulton garage — how do I get it out?
faqs.a: Open garage door fully at dusk, turn off lights, leave area, check back in 30-60 minutes. Usually leaves on its own.

faqs.q: How do I keep opossums away?
faqs.a: Eliminate outdoor pet food, secure garbage, close garage doors at night, seal accessible crawlspaces.

faqs.q: Is there a dead opossum smell in my South Fulton home?
faqs.a: Common call. Recovery $300-$700 depending on access.

mole-removal.metaTitle: Mole Removal South Fulton, GA | Lawn Tunnel Trapping | 24/7
mole-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 mole removal South Fulton, GA — lawn tunneling. Trapping-based. $200-$1,000.
mole-removal.intro: Mole removal calls in South Fulton peak in spring and fall. Eastern moles tunnel through South Fulton lawns. Typical South Fulton mole removal runs $200-$1,000 (multi-acre semi-rural higher).
mole-removal.extendedBody:
Mole vs Vole ID

  • Mole: raised tunnel ridges PLUS volcano-shaped molehills. Doesn't eat plants.
  • Vole: surface runways PLUS chewed shrub bark.

Cost

Sonic stakes, castor oil, mothballs do NOT work. Only trapping in active tunnels works. Cost: $200-$350 single mole, $350-$700 multi-mole, $700-$1,000 multi-acre semi-rural with ongoing trap-and-monitor.

faqs.q: How much does mole removal cost in South Fulton?
faqs.a: $200-$1,000. Single-mole $200-$350. Multi-mole $350-$700. Multi-acre semi-rural with ongoing service $700-$1,000.

faqs.q: How do I tell mole damage from vole damage?
faqs.a: Moles: raised tunnel ridges PLUS cone soil mounds. Voles: flattened surface tracks PLUS chewed shrub bark.

faqs.q: Do mole repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Only trapping works.

faqs.q: How long does mole removal take?
faqs.a: 1-3 visits over 1-2 weeks.

faqs.q: Are moles dangerous?
faqs.a: No. Don't bite, don't carry significant zoonotic disease, don't eat plants.

dead-animal-removal.intro: Dead animal removal in South Fulton spans residential attic recovery, dead-rat work after rodenticide, and barn/outbuilding carcass recovery on semi-rural properties. Typical South Fulton dead animal removal runs $200-$1,500+.
dead-animal-removal.extendedBody:
Locating the Dead Animal

  • Walk each room slowly, sniffing low at baseboards, ceiling height, near HVAC vents.
  • Check attic, crawlspace, outbuildings.
  • Listen for blowflies — buzzing in walls = active maggot activity.

Cost

$200-$350 accessible carcass, $350-$700 wall-cavity recovery, $700-$1,500 multi-animal die-off or under-house/barn recovery, $1,500-$5,000+ full structural remediation.

relatedLinks.anchor: Fulton County dead animal removal hub

faqs.q: How much does dead animal removal cost in South Fulton?
faqs.a: $200-$1,500+. Accessible $200-$350. Wall-cavity $350-$700. Multi-animal die-off or under-house/barn $700-$1,500. Full structural remediation $1,500-$5,000+.

faqs.q: How do I find the dead animal smell?
faqs.a: Walk each room sniffing low at baseboards, ceiling height. Smell strongest at wall closest to carcass. Listen for blowflies.

faqs.q: How long does dead animal smell last?
faqs.a: Severe odor 7-14 days. Decomposition-fluid saturation produces residual odor 1-6 months.

faqs.q: There are flies in my South Fulton house — is it dead-animal related?
faqs.a: Probably yes if blowflies (large metallic green/blue, 1/4-1/2 inch). Adult emergence 7-10 days after death event.

faqs.q: Can I just leave the dead animal?
faqs.a: Decomposes in 3-6 weeks but waiting costs much more in odor, flies, drywall replacement. Same-day removal is dramatically cheaper.

raccoon-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 raccoon removal Union City, GA — Old Union City, Roosevelt Highway, Camp Creek extension subdivisions. $400-$1,500.
raccoon-removal.intro: Raccoon removal calls in Union City split between residential subdivisions and commercial/warehouse-corridor properties. Female raccoons (Procyon lotor) whelp late February through early May. Heaviest pressure: Bear Creek tributary properties and Camp Creek extension subdivisions. Typical Union City raccoon removal runs $400-$1,500.
raccoon-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Raccoons in Your Union City Home

Heavy thumping or chittering between dusk and dawn is the clearest sign. Other Union City signs: damaged soffit corners, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near downspouts, urine smell penetrating ceiling drywall. Baby crying sounds late February-May = raccoon kits.

Union City Submarkets + Cost

  • Old Union City / Roosevelt Highway — older mid-century homes with multi-entry profiles.
  • Camp Creek extension subdivisions — newer homes with vinyl-soffit chew-throughs.
  • Bear Creek corridor properties — continuous source-population pressure.

Cost: $400-$700 single-entry, $700-$1,200 multi-entry or kit-season, $1,200-$1,500+ multi-entry with insulation contamination, $1,500-$3,500 full attic restoration.

faqs.q: How much does raccoon removal cost in Union City, GA?
faqs.a: $400-$1,500. Single-entry $400-$700. Multi-entry or kit-season $700-$1,200. Multi-entry with insulation contamination $1,200-$1,500+. Full attic restoration $1,500-$3,500.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have raccoons in my Union City attic?
faqs.a: Heavy thumping/chittering at dusk/dawn (raccoons weigh 10-25 lbs). Damaged fascia/soffits, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near entry points, urine smell. Entry holes 4-6 inches across.

faqs.q: When can I evict raccoons from my Union City attic?
faqs.a: Female raccoons whelp late February through early May, kits dependent 8-10 weeks. Right approach: one-way doors after kits are mobile, or den-side hand recovery for very young kits.

faqs.q: Are raccoons common along Bear Creek in Union City?
faqs.a: Yes — Bear Creek corridor properties take continuous source-population pressure year-round.

faqs.q: Will the raccoons come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion uses welded steel mesh, hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

squirrel-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 squirrel removal Union City, GA — attic exclusion + chewed-wire repair. $300-$1,200.
squirrel-removal.intro: Squirrel removal calls in Union City run year-round. Eastern gray squirrels (Sciurus carolinensis) dominate. Twin breeding peaks (February-March, August-September). Chewed-wire fire risk in older Roosevelt Highway corridor housing. Typical Union City squirrel removal runs $300-$1,200.
squirrel-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Squirrels in Your Union City Attic

  • Fast scampering during daylight (especially dawn and late afternoon).
  • Chewed entry holes 2-3 inches at soffit corners.
  • Acorn shells on roof or in gutters.
  • Chewed wires — urgent in older housing.

Cost

Older Union City housing has wiring more vulnerable to chew damage. Any chewed Romex requires licensed-electrician follow-up. Cost: $300-$500 single-entry modern, $500-$900 multi-entry, $900-$1,200 with electrician follow-up.

faqs.q: How much does squirrel removal cost in Union City?
faqs.a: $300-$1,200. Single-entry modern $300-$500. Multi-entry $500-$900. Older housing with chewed-wire electrician follow-up $900-$1,200.

faqs.q: How do I tell squirrels from rats in my Union City attic?
faqs.a: Squirrels diurnal (dawn/late afternoon active). Rats nocturnal. Squirrel droppings rice-grain-sized; rat droppings smaller along travel routes.

faqs.q: When can I evict squirrels from my Union City attic?
faqs.a: Two safe windows: May-early June (after first-litter dispersal) and October-November (after second-litter mobile).

faqs.q: Are squirrels really a fire risk in Union City older homes?
faqs.a: Yes — chewed Romex is a leading cause of attic-origin fires. Older Union City housing has more vulnerable wiring.

faqs.q: Will squirrels come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion uses hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

rat-removal.extendedBody:
Norway Rat vs Roof Rat ID

  • Norway rats: blunt 3/4-inch droppings, basement/crawlspace activity, foundation burrows. Common along I-85 industrial corridor.
  • Roof rats: pointed 1/2-inch droppings, attic/ceiling activity. Common in canopy-dense Union City subdivisions.

Cost + Commercial Work

Cost: $500-$800 single-source residential, $800-$1,200 established residential with full exclusion, $1,500-$3,000 commercial/warehouse Norway rat abatement along I-85 corridor.

faqs.q: How much does rat removal cost in Union City?
faqs.a: $500-$2,000. Single-source residential $500-$800. Established residential with full exclusion $800-$1,200. Commercial/warehouse Norway rat abatement along I-85 $1,500-$3,000.

faqs.q: Do I have Norway rats or roof rats in my Union City home?
faqs.a: Activity in basement/crawlspace = Norway rats. Activity in attic/ceiling = roof rats. Pointed half-inch droppings = roof rats; blunt 3/4-inch = Norway rats.

faqs.q: Do you handle commercial rat work along I-85 in Union City?
faqs.a: Yes — commercial Norway rat abatement on warehouse, distribution, and industrial properties is a core Union City service.

faqs.q: When are rats worst in Union City?
faqs.a: Activity peaks October-December. Secondary spike early spring.

faqs.q: Why do rats keep returning?
faqs.a: Almost always because entry points aren't sealed. Durable resolution requires structural exclusion combined with trapping.

bat-removal.metaTitle: Bat Removal Union City, GA | Maternity Compliant | 24/7
bat-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 bat removal Union City, GA — chimney colonies in older Roosevelt Highway corridor housing. $1,500-$4,000.
bat-removal.intro: Bat removal calls in Union City concentrate in older Roosevelt Highway corridor housing. Big brown bats (Eptesicus fuscus) form colonies in chimney chases. Legal exclusion calendar: April or September through mid-October only. Typical Union City bat removal runs $1,500-$4,000.
bat-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Bats in Your Union City Home

  • Dusk emergence — bats stream out 20-30 min after sunset.
  • Brown guano staining on siding below entry.
  • Guano piles on porch or attic floor.

Maternity Calendar + Cost

Safe exclusion windows: April or September through mid-October only. May-August traps non-flying pups. Cost: $1,500-$2,500 modest colony, $2,500-$4,000 multi-decade with HEPA guano remediation.

faqs.q: How much does bat removal cost in Union City?
faqs.a: $1,500-$4,000. Modest colony $1,500-$2,500. Multi-decade with HEPA guano remediation $2,500-$4,000. Trapping bats is essentially banned in Georgia.

faqs.q: What do I do if a bat is inside my Union City home tonight?
faqs.a: If bat in living space and any person/pet was in room while loose — CDC treats as potential rabies exposure. Capture (don't release) for testing. Confine to one room, call Fulton County Board of Health within 24 hours.

faqs.q: When can bat exclusion be done in Union City?
faqs.a: April or September through mid-October only. May-August is maternity season — exclusion traps non-flying pups.

faqs.q: Is bat guano dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes — supports Histoplasma capsulatum (histoplasmosis). Long-established colonies produce inches of accumulated guano. Professional HEPA-equipped decontamination required.

faqs.q: Why can't I do bat removal myself?
faqs.a: Georgia DNR regulations restrict exclusion during May-August maternity season. All exclusion uses one-way valves, not trapping.

Where Snakes Show Up in Union City

Copperhead: coppery-tan with hourglass crossbands, triangular head, 2-3 feet typical. Photo from 10+ feet for ID. ER immediately if bitten. Cost: $150-$250 non-venomous, $250-$400 copperhead, $400-$800 multi-snake.

faqs.q: Are copperheads common in Union City yards?
faqs.a: Yes — documented along Bear Creek corridor and in older Roosevelt Highway corridor yards. Photo from 10+ feet for ID.

faqs.q: I just got bitten by a snake in Union City — what do I do?
faqs.a: ER immediately. Atlanta-area ERs stock antivenom. Do NOT apply ice, cut, suck venom, tourniquet.

faqs.q: When are snakes most active in Union City?
faqs.a: April-October, peaks May-June (mating) and August-September (juvenile dispersal).

faqs.q: Do snake repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Only habitat modification works.

mole-removal.extendedBody:
Mole vs Vole ID

  • Mole: raised tunnel ridges PLUS volcano-shaped molehills. Doesn't eat plants.
  • Vole: surface runways PLUS chewed shrub bark.

Cost

Sonic stakes, castor oil, mothballs do NOT work. Only trapping in active tunnels works. Cost: $200-$350 single mole, $350-$600 multi-mole, $600-$1,500 multi-acre.

faqs.q: How much does mole removal cost in Union City?
How do I tell mole damage from vole damage?
faqs.a: Moles: raised tunnel ridges PLUS cone soil mounds. Voles: flattened surface tracks PLUS chewed shrub bark.

faqs.q: Do mole repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Only trapping works.

faqs.q: How long does mole removal take?
faqs.a: 1-3 visits over 1-2 weeks.

faqs.q: Are moles dangerous?
faqs.a: No. Don't bite, don't carry significant zoonotic disease, don't eat plants.

faqs.q: How do I find the dead animal smell?

faqs.q: How long does dead animal smell last?
faqs.a: Severe odor 7-14 days. Decomposition-fluid saturation produces residual odor 1-6 months.

faqs.q: There are flies in my Union City house — is it dead-animal related?

faqs.q: Can I just leave the dead animal?

raccoon-removal.extendedBody:
Fairburn Submarkets

  • Historic Downtown Fairburn — pre-1940 housing with multi-entry profiles (4-5+ per property), masonry chimneys.
  • Bohannon Road / Ronald Reagan Parkway subdivisions — 1990s-2010s+ vinyl-soffit and chase-cap entries.
  • Eastern Fairburn equestrian area — multi-structure properties (main house + barns + outbuildings).

How to Tell + Cost

Heavy thumping or chittering between dusk and dawn. Damaged soffits, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near downspouts, urine smell. Cost: $400-$700 single-entry, $700-$1,200 multi-entry or kit-season, $1,200-$1,500 historic-downtown multi-entry, $1,500-$2,000+ multi-structure equestrian.

faqs.q: How much does raccoon removal cost in Fairburn, GA?
faqs.a: $400-$2,000. Single-entry $400-$700. Multi-entry or kit-season $700-$1,200. Historic-downtown multi-entry $1,200-$1,500. Multi-structure equestrian $1,500-$2,000+.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have raccoons in my Fairburn attic?
faqs.a: Heavy thumping/chittering at dusk/dawn (raccoons weigh 10-25 lbs). Damaged fascia/soffits, claw marks on downspouts, droppings near entry points, urine smell. Entry holes 4-6 inches across.

faqs.q: When can I evict raccoons from my Fairburn attic?
faqs.a: Female raccoons whelp late February through early May, kits dependent 8-10 weeks. Right approach: one-way doors after kits are mobile, or den-side hand recovery for very young kits.

faqs.q: Do you handle raccoon removal on Fairburn equestrian properties?
faqs.a: Yes — multi-structure work covering main house plus barns, sheds, equipment outbuildings is a Fairburn specialty. Same-day inspection usually available.

faqs.q: Will the raccoons come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion uses welded steel mesh, hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

squirrel-removal.intro: Squirrel removal calls in Fairburn run year-round. Eastern gray squirrels (Sciurus carolinensis) dominate. Twin breeding peaks (February-March, August-September). Chewed-wire fire risk in pre-1940 Historic Downtown housing. Typical Fairburn squirrel removal runs $300-$1,500.
squirrel-removal.extendedBody:
How to Tell If You Have Squirrels in Your Fairburn Attic

  • Fast scampering during daylight (especially dawn and late afternoon).
  • Chewed entry holes 2-3 inches at soffit corners.
  • Acorn shells on roof or in gutters.
  • Chewed wires — urgent in pre-1940 historic-downtown housing.

Cost

Pre-1940 Fairburn historic-downtown housing has wiring more vulnerable to chew damage. Any chewed Romex requires licensed-electrician follow-up. Cost: $300-$500 single-entry modern, $500-$900 multi-entry, $900-$1,500 historic-downtown with electrician follow-up.

faqs.q: How much does squirrel removal cost in Fairburn?
faqs.a: $300-$1,500. Single-entry modern $300-$500. Multi-entry $500-$900. Historic-downtown with electrician follow-up $900-$1,500.

faqs.q: How do I tell squirrels from rats in my Fairburn attic?
faqs.a: Squirrels diurnal (dawn/late afternoon active). Rats nocturnal. Squirrel droppings rice-grain-sized; rat droppings smaller.

faqs.q: When can I evict squirrels from my Fairburn attic?
faqs.a: Two safe windows: May-early June and October-November.

faqs.q: Are squirrels really a fire risk in Fairburn historic homes?
faqs.a: Yes — chewed Romex is a leading cause of attic-origin fires. Pre-1940 Fairburn housing has older wiring more vulnerable to chew damage.

faqs.q: Will squirrels come back?
faqs.a: Only if entry points aren't sealed. Proper exclusion uses hardware cloth, sheet metal — not foam or screen.

rat-removal.extendedBody:
Norway Rat vs Roof Rat ID

  • Norway rats: blunt 3/4-inch droppings, basement/crawlspace activity. Common Historic Downtown + I-85 corridor.
  • Roof rats: pointed 1/2-inch droppings, attic/ceiling activity. Common in canopy-dense subdivisions.

Cost

Cost: $500-$800 single-source residential, $800-$1,200 established with full exclusion, $1,200-$1,800 mixed-species or commercial.

faqs.q: How much does rat removal cost in Fairburn?
faqs.a: $500-$1,500. Single-source $500-$800. Established with full exclusion $800-$1,200. Mixed-species or commercial $1,200-$1,800.

faqs.q: Do I have Norway rats or roof rats in my Fairburn home?
faqs.a: Activity in basement/crawlspace = Norway rats. Activity in attic/ceiling = roof rats. Pointed half-inch droppings = roof rats; blunt 3/4-inch = Norway rats.

faqs.q: When are rats worst in Fairburn?
faqs.a: Activity peaks October-December. Secondary spike early spring.

faqs.q: Are rats in my Fairburn home dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes — CDC documents 35+ diseases. Most relevant: leptospirosis, salmonellosis, hantavirus.

faqs.q: Why do rats keep returning?
faqs.a: Almost always because entry points aren't sealed. Durable resolution requires structural exclusion combined with trapping.

How to Tell If You Have Bats in Your Fairburn Home or Barn

  • Dusk emergence — bats stream out 20-30 min after sunset.
  • Brown guano staining on siding below entry.
  • Guano piles on porch, attic floor, or barn loft.

Maternity Calendar + Cost

Safe windows: April or September through mid-October only. Cost: $1,500-$2,500 modest colony, $2,500-$4,000 historic-downtown multi-decade, $4,000-$5,000+ multi-structure equestrian (main house + barn).

faqs.q: How much does bat removal cost in Fairburn?
faqs.a: $1,500-$5,000. Modest colony $1,500-$2,500. Historic-downtown multi-decade $2,500-$4,000. Multi-structure equestrian (main house + barn) $4,000-$5,000+. Trapping bats is essentially banned in Georgia.

faqs.q: What do I do if a bat is inside my Fairburn home tonight?
faqs.a: If bat in living space and any person/pet was in room while loose — CDC treats as potential rabies exposure. Capture (don't release) for testing. Confine to one room, call Fulton County Board of Health within 24 hours.

faqs.q: When can bat exclusion be done in Fairburn?
faqs.a: April or September through mid-October only. May-August is maternity season — exclusion traps non-flying pups.

faqs.q: Do you handle bat removal on Fairburn barn properties?
faqs.a: Yes — multi-structure bat work on equestrian and farm properties is a Fairburn specialty. Main house chimney plus barn loft colonies are routine.

faqs.q: Why can't I do bat removal myself?
faqs.a: Georgia DNR regulations restrict exclusion during May-August maternity season. All exclusion uses one-way valves, not trapping.

snake-removal.extendedBody:
Where Snakes Show Up in Fairburn

  • Equestrian estate properties — copperheads in pasture-edge brush, woodpiles, hay storage areas.
  • Bear Creek tributary properties — copperheads in adjacent yards.
  • Bohannon Road / Ronald Reagan Parkway subdivisions — copperheads in mature shrub cover.
  • Historic Downtown Fairburn yards — copperheads in dense ground cover.

Copperhead ID + Equestrian Risk

Copperhead: coppery-tan with hourglass crossbands, triangular head, 2-3 feet typical. Photo from 10+ feet for ID. Horses can be bitten on the muzzle while grazing — vet evaluation required for any equine snake bite. Cost: $150-$250 non-venomous, $250-$500 copperhead, $500-$1,200 multi-snake or property survey on equestrian acreage.

faqs.q: How much does snake removal cost in Fairburn?
faqs.a: $150-$600. Single non-venomous $150-$250. Copperhead $250-$500. Multi-snake or equestrian property survey $500-$1,200.

faqs.q: Are copperheads common on Fairburn equestrian properties?
faqs.a: Yes — common per-property because of pasture-edge brush, woodpiles, hay storage areas. Take a photo from 10+ feet for ID.

faqs.q: What if my horse gets bitten by a copperhead in Fairburn?
faqs.a: Equine snake bites require immediate veterinary evaluation. Horse muzzles are common bite sites during grazing.

faqs.q: I just got bitten by a snake in Fairburn — what do I do?
faqs.a: ER immediately. Atlanta-area ERs stock antivenom. Do NOT apply ice, cut, suck venom, tourniquet.

faqs.q: Do snake repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Mothballs, sulfur, sonic stakes — none reliably deter snakes. Only habitat modification works.

faqs.q: How do I keep groundhogs out of my Fairburn garden or pasture?

faqs.q: Are groundhogs protected by Georgia law?

faqs.a: Baby skunk season April-July. DO NOT seal the den. Den-side hand recovery for kits, then trap mother.

Venomous ID + Equine Risk

Copperhead: coppery-tan with hourglass crossbands, triangular head, 2-3 feet typical. Cottonmouth: heavy-bodied dark olive-to-black, gapes white interior of mouth defensively, near water. Photo from 10+ feet for ID. Horses can be bitten on the muzzle while grazing — vet evaluation required for any equine snake bite. Cost: $150-$250 non-venomous, $250-$500 copperhead, $300-$600 cottonmouth, $500-$1,200 multi-snake or property survey.

faqs.q: How much does snake removal cost in Palmetto?
faqs.a: $150-$700. Single non-venomous $150-$250. Copperhead $250-$500. Cottonmouth $300-$600. Multi-snake or property survey $500-$1,200.

faqs.q: Are copperheads or cottonmouths common in Palmetto?
faqs.a: Copperheads very common per-property because of rural land use. Cottonmouths appear near Chattahoochee tributaries, ponds, wet/marshy areas. Take a photo from 10+ feet for ID.

faqs.q: What if my horse gets bitten by a copperhead in Palmetto?
faqs.a: Equine snake bites require immediate veterinary evaluation. Horse muzzles are common bite sites during grazing.

faqs.q: I just got bitten by a snake in Palmetto — what do I do?
faqs.a: ER immediately. Atlanta-area ERs stock antivenom. Do NOT apply ice, cut, suck venom, tourniquet.

faqs.q: Do snake repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Only habitat modification works (clearing brush, removing woodpiles, securing hay storage).

Groundhog Burrow ID on Palmetto Farm Properties

  • Burrow entrance 8-12 inches with fan-shaped fresh dirt mound.
  • Common Palmetto locations: under barn outbuildings, equipment sheds, run-in shelters, farm porches.
  • Adult groundhog: 16-26 inches, 5-13 lbs.
  • Garden + crop damage: clean-cut bites of beans, peas, lettuce, vegetable rows.

Multi-Structure Cost

Female groundhogs whelp April-May; kits dependent until late July/early August. Cost: $250-$400 single accessible burrow, $400-$700 kit-season hand recovery, $700-$1,500 multi-burrow farm property (main house + barns + outbuildings). Burrow remediation $200-$600 per system.

faqs.q: How much does groundhog removal cost in Palmetto?
faqs.a: $250-$1,500. Single accessible burrow $250-$400. Kit-season hand recovery $400-$700. Multi-burrow farm property (main house + barns + outbuildings) $700-$1,500.

faqs.q: Will a groundhog burrow damage my Palmetto barn or shed?
faqs.a: Yes within 1-3 years. Tunnel systems run 25-30 feet long; excavation removes soil supporting barn slabs and equipment-shed foundations. Repair $500-$5,000+.

faqs.q: When do groundhogs have babies in Palmetto?
faqs.a: April-May. Litters 2-6 kits; dependent until late July/early August. Kit-season hand recovery required.

faqs.q: How do I keep groundhogs out of my Palmetto garden?
faqs.a: Heavy-gauge welded-wire fencing extending 12 inches below grade and 36 inches above grade, top loose-bent outward.

faqs.q: Are groundhogs protected by Georgia law?
faqs.a: Not protected as game species. Commercial trapping requires Georgia DNR Trapping License.

Rabies signs: daytime activity, tame behavior, circling, drooling. Bite/scratch/saliva exposure requires physician evaluation within 24 hours. Pet exposure (dogs, barn cats) requires immediate vet evaluation.

Spray formula: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Cost: $300-$450 single live-trap, $450-$700 multi-skunk family with kit-season hand recovery, $700-$1,000 multi-burrow farm property (main house + barn + outbuildings), $200-$800 spray remediation separate.

faqs.q: How much does skunk removal cost in Palmetto?

faqs.q: How do I get skunk smell out of my dog?
faqs.a: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Apply fresh, work 5 min, rinse.

faqs.q: How do I keep opossums away from my Palmetto property?

faqs.q: Is there a dead opossum smell in my Palmetto barn?

Chattahoochee Hills bat colonies routinely span main house plus barn lofts plus equestrian outbuildings. Timber rattler: large (3-5 feet), gray-tan with dark V-shaped crossbands, rattle. Photo from 10+ feet for ID. Horses can be bitten on the muzzle while grazing — vet evaluation required for any equine snake bite. faqs.q: Are venomous snakes really common in Chattahoochee Hills?

faqs.a: Not protected as game species. Commercial trapping requires Georgia DNR Trapping License.

bird-removal.metaTitle: Bird Removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA | Barn + Residential | 24/7
bird-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 bird removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA — barn-property pigeons, residential starlings, Serenbe area. $400-$5,000.
bird-removal.intro: Bird removal in Chattahoochee Hills focuses on barn-property pigeon and starling work on equestrian/farm properties, plus residential starling vent nests in Serenbe area homes. Pigeons, starlings, sparrows are non-native and not MBTA-protected. Conservation-area native species require federal depredation permits. Typical Chattahoochee Hills bird removal runs $400-$5,000.
bird-removal.extendedBody:
Chattahoochee Hills Bird Pest Pattern

  • Barn-property pigeons (feed access concentrates them).
  • Barn-property starlings (rafters, vent systems).
  • Residential starling vent nests in Serenbe + estate homes.
  • MBTA-protected native species (extensive in conservation-area habitat) require federal permit.

Cost

$400-$600 single residential nest, $1,500-$3,000 barn-property pigeon abatement, $3,000-$5,000+ large multi-barn commercial abatement with full HEPA droppings remediation.

faqs.q: How much does bird removal cost in Chattahoochee Hills?
faqs.a: $400-$5,000. Single residential nest $400-$600. Barn-property pigeon abatement $1,500-$3,000. Large multi-barn commercial $3,000-$5,000+.

faqs.q: Are pigeons protected by federal law?
faqs.a: No. Rock pigeons, starlings, sparrows are non-native and NOT MBTA-protected. Conservation-area native species ARE MBTA-protected and require federal depredation permits.

faqs.q: Pigeons are roosting in my Chattahoochee Hills barn — what do I do?
faqs.a: HEPA droppings remediation + exclusion (netting in rafters, spikes, blocking access points). $1,500-$3,000 typical.

faqs.q: How do I get rid of starlings in my Serenbe-area dryer vent?
faqs.a: Remove nest material, install vent guard with louvered cap, treat droppings. $400-$600.

faqs.q: Are bird droppings on my barn dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes with significant accumulation. Histoplasmosis exposure during cleanup. Professional HEPA-equipped remediation mandatory.

skunk-removal.metaTitle: Skunk Removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA | Spray-Free | 24/7
skunk-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 skunk removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA — under-deck/barn dens on conservation-area estates. Rabies vector. $300-$1,500.
skunk-removal.intro: Skunk removal calls in Chattahoochee Hills run February-July. Striped skunks den under decks, barn outbuildings, sheds, pool houses on multi-acre estates. Skunks are rabies-vector species. Multi-structure denning is the norm. Typical Chattahoochee Hills skunk removal runs $300-$1,500.
skunk-removal.extendedBody:
Multi-Structure Skunk Denning in Chattahoochee Hills

  • Persistent musky odor under decks, barn outbuildings, sheds, pool houses.
  • Burrow entrance 6-8 inches.
  • Cone-shaped lawn or pasture digs.
  • Skunk visible at dawn or dusk.

Rabies signs: daytime activity, tame behavior, circling, drooling. Bite/scratch/saliva exposure requires physician evaluation within 24 hours.

Cost

Spray formula: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Cost: $300-$450 single live-trap, $450-$700 multi-skunk family with kit-season hand recovery, $700-$1,500 multi-burrow estate (main house + barn + outbuildings + pool house), $200-$800 spray remediation separate.

faqs.q: How much does skunk removal cost in Chattahoochee Hills?
faqs.a: $300-$1,500. Single live-trap $300-$450. Multi-skunk with kit-season hand recovery $450-$700. Multi-burrow estate $700-$1,500. Spray remediation $200-$800 separate.

faqs.q: How do I get skunk smell out of my dog?
faqs.a: 1 quart 3% hydrogen peroxide + 1/4 cup baking soda + 1 teaspoon dish soap. Apply fresh, work 5 min, rinse.

faqs.q: Is the skunk on my Chattahoochee Hills property rabid?
faqs.a: Possibly — Georgia is rabies-endemic. Daytime activity, tame behavior, circling, drooling are warning signs.

faqs.q: There are baby skunks under my Chattahoochee Hills barn — what do I do?
faqs.a: Baby skunk season April-July. DO NOT seal the den. Den-side hand recovery for kits, then trap mother.

faqs.q: Can I trap a skunk myself?
faqs.a: Strongly not recommended. Spray triggers easily. Bite/scratch risk is real.

opossum-removal.metaTitle: Opossum Removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA | Humane | 24/7
opossum-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 opossum removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA — barn outbuildings, garages, decks. Low rabies. $200-$700.
opossum-removal.intro: Opossum removal calls in Chattahoochee Hills run year-round. Virginia opossums (Didelphis virginiana) are well-established across the city's conservation-area properties and equestrian estates. Body temperature (94-97°F) is below rabies-optimal range. Typical Chattahoochee Hills opossum removal runs $200-$700.
opossum-removal.extendedBody:
Opossum Sightings on Chattahoochee Hills Estates

  • Common locations: under porches/decks, in barn outbuildings, in garages, eating barn-cat food.
  • Behavior: nocturnal, slow, plays dead.
  • Property damage: scattered trash, eaten pet/barn-cat food, flea/tick concentrations under multi-year denning.

Cost

$200-$300 single live-trap, $300-$500 mother + joeys (6-13), $500-$1,000 multi-opossum + flea/tick remediation, $300-$700 dead opossum recovery from barn or under-house.

faqs.q: How much does opossum removal cost in Chattahoochee Hills?
faqs.a: $200-$700. Single live-trap $200-$300. Mother + joeys $300-$500. Multi-opossum + flea/tick $500-$1,000. Dead opossum recovery $300-$700.

faqs.q: Do opossums carry rabies?
faqs.a: Very rarely. Body temperature below rabies-optimal range. CDC treats opossum bites as low-risk.

faqs.q: There's an opossum in my Chattahoochee Hills barn — how do I get it out?
faqs.a: Open the door fully at dusk, turn off lights, leave area, check back in 30-60 minutes. Usually leaves on its own.

faqs.q: How do I keep opossums away from my Chattahoochee Hills property?
faqs.a: Eliminate outdoor pet food, secure barn-cat food, secure garbage, close garage/barn doors at night, seal accessible crawlspaces.

faqs.q: Is there a dead opossum smell in my Chattahoochee Hills barn?
faqs.a: Common call. Recovery from barn or under-house spaces $300-$700.

mole-removal.metaTitle: Mole Removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA | Lawn + Pasture | 24/7
mole-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 mole removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA — multi-acre lawn + pasture tunneling. Trapping-based. $200-$1,500.
mole-removal.intro: Mole removal calls in Chattahoochee Hills peak in spring and fall. Eastern moles tunnel through Chattahoochee Hills lawns and pastures. Multi-acre conservation-area properties often need ongoing trap-and-monitor service. Typical Chattahoochee Hills mole removal runs $200-$1,500.
mole-removal.extendedBody:
Mole vs Vole on Conservation-Area Properties

  • Mole: raised tunnel ridges PLUS volcano-shaped molehills. Doesn't eat plants.
  • Vole: surface runways PLUS chewed shrub bark.

Cost on Multi-Acre Properties

Sonic stakes, castor oil, mothballs do NOT work. Only trapping in active tunnels works. Cost: $200-$350 single mole, $350-$700 multi-mole on larger lawn, $700-$1,500 multi-acre with ongoing trap-and-monitor (typical Chattahoochee Hills estate scope).

faqs.q: How much does mole removal cost in Chattahoochee Hills?
faqs.a: $200-$1,500. Single-mole $200-$350. Multi-mole on larger lawn $350-$700. Multi-acre conservation-area with ongoing trap-and-monitor $700-$1,500.

faqs.q: How do I tell mole damage from vole damage?
faqs.a: Moles: raised tunnel ridges PLUS cone soil mounds. Voles: flattened surface tracks PLUS chewed shrub bark.

faqs.q: Do mole repellents work?
faqs.a: No. Only trapping works.

faqs.q: How long does mole removal take on a Chattahoochee Hills estate?
faqs.a: Single-mole removal takes 1-3 visits over 1-2 weeks. Multi-acre estate properties may need ongoing trap-and-monitor service for several months.

faqs.q: Are moles dangerous to horses or livestock?
faqs.a: No. Don't bite, don't carry significant zoonotic disease, don't damage pasture grass directly.

dead-animal-removal.metaTitle: Dead Animal Removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA | Same-Day | 24/7
dead-animal-removal.metaDescription: 24/7 dead animal removal Chattahoochee Hills, GA — attic, barn, dead-livestock, coyote-related dead pets. $200-$1,500.
dead-animal-removal.intro: Dead animal removal in Chattahoochee Hills spans residential attic recovery to barn carcass work, dead-livestock disposal, and coyote-related dead-pet recovery on conservation-area properties. Typical Chattahoochee Hills dead animal removal runs $200-$1,500+, with same-day service standard.
dead-animal-removal.extendedBody:
Locating the Dead Animal on Chattahoochee Hills Multi-Structure Properties

  • Walk each room slowly, sniffing low at baseboards, ceiling height, near HVAC vents.
  • Check attic, crawlspace, barn lofts, equipment outbuildings, run-in shelters.
  • Listen for blowflies — buzzing in walls = active maggot activity.

Cost + Coyote-Related Recovery

$200-$350 accessible carcass, $350-$700 wall-cavity recovery, $700-$1,500 multi-animal die-off or under-house/barn recovery, $1,500-$5,000+ full structural remediation. Dead-livestock disposal on Chattahoochee Hills farms and coyote-related dead-pet recovery on acreage are separate services.

relatedLinks.anchor: Fulton County dead animal removal hub

faqs.q: How much does dead animal removal cost in Chattahoochee Hills?
faqs.a: $200-$1,500+. Accessible $200-$350. Wall-cavity $350-$700. Multi-animal die-off or under-house/barn recovery $700-$1,500. Full structural remediation $1,500-$5,000+.

faqs.q: Do you handle dead-livestock disposal in Chattahoochee Hills?
faqs.a: Yes — dead-livestock disposal on Chattahoochee Hills farms is offered as a separate service. Coyote-related dead-pet recovery on conservation acreage is also covered.

faqs.q: How do I find the dead animal smell in my Chattahoochee Hills home?
faqs.a: Walk each room sniffing low at baseboards, ceiling height. Smell strongest at wall closest to carcass. Listen for blowflies.

faqs.q: How long does dead animal smell last?
faqs.a: Severe odor 7-14 days. Decomposition-fluid saturation produces residual odor 1-6 months.

faqs.q: Can I just leave the dead animal?
faqs.a: Decomposes in 3-6 weeks but waiting costs much more. Same-day removal is dramatically cheaper.
